paper_threads.summary: Introduces Hyperagents, an AI system that improves at solving tasks and also improves how it improves itself through the Darwin Gödel Machine (DGM), demonstrating open-ended self-improvement.
The AI Scientist paper is now published in Nature, featuring scaling law results showing how it improves with more compute and more intelligent systems for fully automated AI research.
Presents OmniReset, a framework for training robot policies using large-scale RL and diverse resets for contact-rich manipulation tasks without demos or reward engineering.
Introduces Multi-Answer RL, a method that trains language models to produce diverse plausible answers rather than collapsing to single responses, addressing mode collapse in RL training.
Shares PivotRL paper for high accuracy agentic post-training at low compute cost, one of the techniques used for Nemotron-3 post-training.
Presents scaling law analysis showing bias in parabolic IsoFLOP fits used by major companies, with potential for millions in misallocated compute at frontier scales.
Introduces MolmoWeb, built from scratch with Molmo2, releasing SOTA web agents with open source VLMs including all models and datasets.

announcements.summary: Announces Gemini 3.1 Flash Live, their highest quality audio & voice model with lower latency and better precision for voice-first agents, available in Gemini Live.
Launches Safety Bug Bounty program focused on identifying AI abuse and safety risks across OpenAI products, expanding beyond security to include AI-specific safety issues.
Releases Cohere Transcribe, a SOTA open source transcription model running in browser, ranking #1 on Hugging Face Open ASR Leaderboard.
Announces partnership with Agile Robots to deploy Google's models through their platform to solve complex industrial challenges.
Showcases new Lyria 3 Pro music generation capabilities with perfect background music for flow state, available to Google AI subscribers in Gemini App.
Announces ARC-AGI-3, described as the only unsaturated agentic intelligence benchmark where humans score 100% but AI scores less than 1%.

discussions.summary: Observes that RL is the dominant paradigm in AI and everyone designs around it, but old school RL people are mostly uninterested and sitting out the LLM stage.
Discusses the irony that while LLMs are supposedly great for learning, people often conclude there's no point since LLMs already do better than humans.
Discusses how Cursor trains one Composer 2 checkpoint every 5 hours, showing early signs of 'continual learning' in production systems.
Questions NetHack being overlooked as an unsaturated agentic intelligence benchmark, pointing out it has been unsaturated since 2020.
Expresses concern about the end of an era for AI2, noting they are a favorite lab with pure love for the game and immense kindness.
